model: ProofCast editorial (composed from TxLINE-verified events + web2 names)
text:
What a night in the World Cup! Round of 16, 5 July 2026, East Rutherford — and Erling Haaland dragged Norway to their first-ever World Cup quarter-final and sent five-time winners Brazil crashing out. Norway had never been this far; Brazil had never gone home this early since 1990. History swung on a single, breathless finish.

79' — Erling Haaland buried it, 0–1! 90' — Erling Haaland lashed it home, 0–2! 100' — Neymar from the spot made no mistake, 1–2!

Goalkeeper Ørjan Nyland had already produced a sensational display, saving a first-half penalty from Bruno Guimarães to keep the tie level. Then Haaland took over, striking twice in the final eleven minutes — his sixth and seventh goals of the tournament — to stun the favourites. Neymar's stoppage-time penalty was little more than a consolation for a Brazil side that had run out of ideas. For a Norwegian generation long told this could not be done, Haaland was busy proving himself wrong.

You could feel the tension crackle around the ground with every attack.

This is knockout football at its rawest — win, or fly home.

The neutrals were on their feet; nobody in the building sat still.

FULL-TIME: Brazil 1–2 Norway! Norway march on, Brazil head home heartbroken — and here's the beautiful part: every goal, every minute you just heard is locked on-chain and verified by TxLINE. No hot air, no guesswork, just receipts. What. A. Game!
